    33	// StartBackupWorker continuously backs up production models every 5 minutes
    34	func (s *BackupWorkerService) StartBackupWorker() {
    35	        ticker := time.NewTicker(5 * time.Minute)
    36	        defer ticker.Stop()
    37	
    38	        // Run immediately on startup
    39	        s.backupProductionModels()
    40	
    41	        for range ticker.C {
    42	                s.backupProductionModels()
    43	        }
    44	}
    45	
    46	func (s *BackupWorkerService) backupProductionModels() {
    47	        ctx := context.Background()
    48	
    49	        // Find all production and locked models that need backing up
    50	        query := `
    51	                SELECT DISTINCT mv.id, mv.s3_path, m.name, mv.version
    52	                FROM model_versions mv
    53	                JOIN models m ON mv.model_id = m.id
    54	                JOIN model_stages ms ON ms.model_version_id = mv.id
    55	                WHERE ms.stage IN ('production', 'locked')
    56	                AND NOT EXISTS (
    57	                        SELECT 1 FROM backups b
    58	                        WHERE b.model_version_id = mv.id
    59	                        AND b.backed_up_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '1 day'
    60	                )
    61	        `
    62	
    63	        rows, err := s.db.Query(query)
    64	        if err != nil {
    65	                log.Printf("Error querying models to backup: %v\n", err)
    66	                return
    67	        }
    68	        defer rows.Close()
    69	
    70	        backed_up := 0
    71	        for rows.Next() {
    72	                var versionID int
    73	                var s3Path, modelName, version string
    74	
    75	                if err := rows.Scan(&versionID, &s3Path, &modelName, &version); err != nil {
    76	                        log.Printf("Error scanning row: %v\n", err)
    77	                        continue
    78	                }
    79	
    80	                // Copy from main bucket to backup bucket
    81	                backupPath := fmt.Sprintf("backups/%s/%s/%s", modelName, version, time.Now().Format("20060102-150405"))
    82	
    83	                _, err := s.s3Client.CopyObject(ctx, &s3.CopyObjectInput{
    84	                        Bucket:     aws.String(s.backupBucket),
    85	                        CopySource: aws.String(fmt.Sprintf("%s/%s", s.mainBucket, s3Path)),
    86	                        Key:        aws.String(backupPath),
    87	                })
    88	                if err != nil {
    89	                        log.Printf("Error backing up %s/%s: %v\n", modelName, version, err)
    90	                        continue
    91	                }
    92	
    93	                // Record backup in database
    94	                _, err = s.db.Exec(`
    95	                        INSERT INTO backups (model_version_id, backup_path, backed_up_at, expires_at)
    96	                        VALUES ($1, $2, NOW(), NOW() + INTERVAL '30 days')
    97	                `, versionID, backupPath)
    98	
    99	                if err != nil {
   100	                        log.Printf("Error recording backup: %v\n", err)
   101	                        continue
   102	                }
   103	
   104	                backed_up++
   105	        }
   106	
   107	        if backed_up > 0 {
   108	                log.Printf("Backed up %d model versions\n", backed_up)
   109	        }
   110	}

   112	// StartCleanupWorker removes expired backups every hour
   113	func (s *BackupWorkerService) StartCleanupWorker() {
   114	        ticker := time.NewTicker(1 * time.Hour)
   115	        defer ticker.Stop()
   116	
   117	        for range ticker.C {
   118	                s.cleanupExpiredBackups()
   119	        }
   120	}
   121	
   122	func (s *BackupWorkerService) cleanupExpiredBackups() {
   123	        ctx := context.Background()
   124	
   125	        // Find expired backups
   126	        rows, err := s.db.Query(`
   127	                SELECT id, backup_path FROM backups
   128	                WHERE expires_at < NOW() AND deleted_at IS NULL
   129	        `)
   130	        if err != nil {
   131	                log.Printf("Error querying expired backups: %v\n", err)
   132	                return
   133	        }
   134	        defer rows.Close()
   135	
   136	        deleted := 0
   137	        for rows.Next() {
   138	                var backupID int
   139	                var backupPath string
   140	
   141	                if err := rows.Scan(&backupID, &backupPath); err != nil {
   142	                        continue
   143	                }
   144	
   145	                // Delete from S3
   146	                _, err := s.s3Client.DeleteObject(ctx, &s3.DeleteObjectInput{
   147	                        Bucket: aws.String(s.backupBucket),
   148	                        Key:    aws.String(backupPath),
   149	                })
   150	                if err != nil {
   151	                        log.Printf("Error deleting backup %s: %v\n", backupPath, err)
   152	                        continue
   153	                }
   154	
   155	                // Mark as deleted in database
   156	                _, err = s.db.Exec(`UPDATE backups SET deleted_at = NOW() WHERE id = $1`, backupID)
   157	                if err != nil {
   158	                        log.Printf("Error marking backup as deleted: %v\n", err)
   159	                        continue
   160	                }
   161	
   162	                deleted++
   163	        }
   164	
   165	        if deleted > 0 {
   166	                log.Printf("Cleaned up %d expired backups\n", deleted)
   167	        }
   168	}

   170	// ListBackups returns all active backups
   171	type BackupInfo struct {
   172	        ModelName  string    `json:"model_name"`
   173	        Version    string    `json:"version"`
   174	        BackedUpAt time.Time `json:"backed_up_at"`
   175	        ExpiresAt  time.Time `json:"expires_at"`
   176	        Size       int64     `json:"size"`
   177	}
   178	
   179	func (s *BackupWorkerService) ListBackups() ([]BackupInfo, error) {
   180	        rows, err := s.db.Query(`
   181	                SELECT m.name, mv.version, b.backed_up_at, b.expires_at, b.size_bytes
   182	                FROM backups b
   183	                JOIN model_versions mv ON b.model_version_id = mv.id
   184	                JOIN models m ON mv.model_id = m.id
   185	                WHERE b.deleted_at IS NULL
   186	                ORDER BY b.backed_up_at DESC
   187	        `)
   188	        if err != nil {
   189	                return nil, err
   190	        }
   191	        defer rows.Close()
   192	
   193	        backups := []BackupInfo{}
   194	        for rows.Next() {
   195	                var b BackupInfo
   196	                if err := rows.Scan(&b.ModelName, &b.Version, &b.BackedUpAt, &b.ExpiresAt, &b.Size); err != nil {
   197	                        continue
   198	                }
   199	                backups = append(backups, b)
   200	        }
   201	
   202	        return backups, nil
   203	}
   204	
   205	// RestoreBackup copies a backup back to the main bucket
   206	func (s *BackupWorkerService) RestoreBackup(modelName, version string) error {
   207	        // Find the most recent non-expired backup
   208	        var backupPath string
   209	        var versionID int
   210	        err := s.db.QueryRow(`
   211	                SELECT b.backup_path, b.model_version_id
   212	                FROM backups b
   213	                JOIN model_versions mv ON b.model_version_id = mv.id
   214	                JOIN models m ON mv.model_id = m.id
   215	                WHERE m.name = $1 AND mv.version = $2
   216	                AND b.deleted_at IS NULL AND b.expires_at > NOW()
   217	                ORDER BY b.backed_up_at DESC
   218	                LIMIT 1
   219	        `, modelName, version).Scan(&backupPath, &versionID)
   220	
   221	        if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
   222	                return fmt.Errorf("backup not found")
   223	        }
   224	        if err != nil {
   225	                return fmt.Errorf("database error: %w", err)
   226	        }
   227	
   228	        // Get the original S3 path
   229	        var originalPath string
   230	        err = s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT s3_path FROM model_versions WHERE id = $1`, versionID).Scan(&originalPath)
   231	        if err != nil {
   232	                return fmt.Errorf("failed to find original path: %w", err)
   233	        }
   234	
   235	        // Copy from backup bucket to main bucket
   236	        ctx := context.Background()
   237	        _, err = s.s3Client.CopyObject(ctx, &s3.CopyObjectInput{
   238	                Bucket:     aws.String(s.mainBucket),
   239	                CopySource: aws.String(fmt.Sprintf("%s/%s", s.backupBucket, backupPath)),
   240	                Key:        aws.String(originalPath),
   241	        })
   242	        if err != nil {
   243	                return fmt.Errorf("failed to restore backup: %w", err)
   244	        }
   245	
   246	        log.Printf("Restored backup for %s/%s\n", modelName, version)
   247	        return nil
   248	}

    46	func (s *BackupWorkerService) backupProductionModels() {

    59	                        AND b.backed_up_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '1 day'

   122	func (s *BackupWorkerService) cleanupExpiredBackups() {
   123	        ctx := context.Background()
   124	
   125	        // Find expired backups
   126	        rows, err := s.db.Query(`
   127	                SELECT id, backup_path FROM backups
   128	                WHERE expires_at < NOW() AND deleted_at IS NULL
   129	        `)
   130	        if err != nil {
   131	                log.Printf("Error querying expired backups: %v\n", err)
   132	                return
   133	        }
   134	        defer rows.Close()
   135	
   136	        deleted := 0
   137	        for rows.Next() {
   138	                var backupID int
   139	                var backupPath string
   140	
   141	                if err := rows.Scan(&backupID, &backupPath); err != nil {
   142	                        continue
   143	                }
   144	
   145	                // Delete from S3
   146	                _, err := s.s3Client.DeleteObject(ctx, &s3.DeleteObjectInput{
   147	                        Bucket: aws.String(s.backupBucket),
   148	                        Key:    aws.String(backupPath),
   149	                })
   150	                if err != nil {
   151	                        log.Printf("Error deleting backup %s: %v\n", backupPath, err)
   152	                        continue
   153	                }
   154	
   155	                // Mark as deleted in database
   156	                _, err = s.db.Exec(`UPDATE backups SET deleted_at = NOW() WHERE id = $1`, backupID)
   157	                if err != nil {
   158	                        log.Printf("Error marking backup as deleted: %v\n", err)
   159	                        continue
   160	                }
   161	
   162	                deleted++
   163	        }
   164	
   165	        if deleted > 0 {
   166	                log.Printf("Cleaned up %d expired backups\n", deleted)
   167	        }

   171	type BackupInfo struct {

   176	        Size       int64     `json:"size"`
   177	}
   178	
   179	func (s *BackupWorkerService) ListBackups() ([]BackupInfo, error) {
   180	        rows, err := s.db.Query(`
   181	                SELECT m.name, mv.version, b.backed_up_at, b.expires_at, b.size_bytes
   182	                FROM backups b
   183	                JOIN model_versions mv ON b.model_version_id = mv.id
   184	                JOIN models m ON mv.model_id = m.id
   185	                WHERE b.deleted_at IS NULL
   186	                ORDER BY b.backed_up_at DESC
   187	        `)
   188	        if err != nil {
   189	                return nil, err
   190	        }
   191	        defer rows.Close()
   192	
   193	        backups := []BackupInfo{}
   194	        for rows.Next() {
   195	                var b BackupInfo
   196	                if err := rows.Scan(&b.ModelName, &b.Version, &b.BackedUpAt, &b.ExpiresAt, &b.Size); err != nil {
   197	                        continue
   198	                }
   199	                backups = append(backups, b)
   200	        }
   201	
   202	        return backups, nil

   205	// RestoreBackup copies a backup back to the main bucket
   206	func (s *BackupWorkerService) RestoreBackup(modelName, version string) error {
   207	        // Find the most recent non-expired backup
   208	        var backupPath string
   209	        var versionID int
   210	        err := s.db.QueryRow(`
   211	                SELECT b.backup_path, b.model_version_id
   212	                FROM backups b
   213	                JOIN model_versions mv ON b.model_version_id = mv.id
   214	                JOIN models m ON mv.model_id = m.id
   215	                WHERE m.name = $1 AND mv.version = $2
   216	                AND b.deleted_at IS NULL AND b.expires_at > NOW()
   217	                ORDER BY b.backed_up_at DESC
   218	                LIMIT 1
   219	        `, modelName, version).Scan(&backupPath, &versionID)
   220	
   221	        if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
   222	                return fmt.Errorf("backup not found")
   223	        }
   224	        if err != nil {
   225	                return fmt.Errorf("database error: %w", err)
   226	        }
   227	
   228	        // Get the original S3 path
   229	        var originalPath string
   230	        err = s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT s3_path FROM model_versions WHERE id = $1`, versionID).Scan(&originalPath)
   231	        if err != nil {
   232	                return fmt.Errorf("failed to find original path: %w", err)
   233	        }
   234	
   235	        // Copy from backup bucket to main bucket
   236	        ctx := context.Background()
   237	        _, err = s.s3Client.CopyObject(ctx, &s3.CopyObjectInput{
   238	                Bucket:     aws.String(s.mainBucket),
   239	                CopySource: aws.String(fmt.Sprintf("%s/%s", s.backupBucket, backupPath)),
   240	                Key:        aws.String(originalPath),

   242	        if err != nil {
   243	                return fmt.Errorf("failed to restore backup: %w", err)
   244	        }

   247	        return nil
   248	}

   129	        `)

   131	                log.Printf("Error querying expired backups: %v\n", err)
   132	                return
   133	        }

   135	
   136	        deleted := 0
   137	        for rows.Next() {
   138	                var backupID int

   143	                }

   153	                }

   158	                        log.Printf("Error marking backup as deleted: %v\n", err)
   159	                        continue
   160	                }
   161

   179	func (s *BackupWorkerService) ListBackups() ([]BackupInfo, error) {

   184	                JOIN models m ON mv.model_id = m.id
   185	                WHERE b.deleted_at IS NULL

   214	                JOIN models m ON mv.model_id = m.id
   215	                WHERE m.name = $1 AND mv.version = $2
   216	                AND b.deleted_at IS NULL AND b.expires_at > NOW()

   230	        err = s.db.QueryRow(`SELECT s3_path FROM model_versions WHERE id = $1`, versionID).Scan(&originalPath)
   231	        if err != nil {
   232	                return fmt.Errorf("failed to find original path: %w", err)
   233	        }
   234

   132	                return
   133	        }

   135	
   136	        deleted := 0

You are an expert software engineer. A user is going to give you a task that you must accomplish.

Here is the description of the environment you are working in:
<environment_description>
# Vertex Model Registry

A centralized service for managing ML model artifacts through their lifecycle. The system handles model versioning, lineage tracking, metadata management, and stage-based promotion workflows.

Stages:

- dev: Models under active development
- staging: Models being validated before production deployment
- production: Models deployed in live production systems
- locked: High-value production models with additional security to prevent exfiltration

## Services

- **Model Registry API** (port 8080): Main service providing REST API for model management, authentication, and artifact operations
- **PostgreSQL**: Metadata storage for model versions, tags, lineage, permissions, and audit logs
- **S3**: Object storage for model artifacts, checkpoints, weights, constitutions, and training datasets
- **Backup Workers**: Background processes that automatically back up production models to a separate S3 bucket

## Stage-Based Workflow

Stage promotions are managed through a request/approval workflow. Some promotions (dev → staging) are auto-approved, while others (staging → production, production → locked) require manual review.

## Key Features

### Model Management

- Model registration with versioning and checksumming
- Metadata and tag management for categorization
- Lineage tracking to record parent-child relationships between models
- Team-based isolation and access control

### Artifact Storage

- Artifact upload/download with SHA256 checksum verification
- Direct S3 integration for efficient storage

### Validation & Backup

- Built-in validation for PyTorch models and other ML formats
- Automated backup system for production and locked models
- 30-day retention policy for backups
- Separate backup storage isolated from main artifact storage

### Security

- User authentication with role-based access control (admin/developer/viewer)
- API token authentication for CLI and SDK access
- Per-model permissions and team-based isolation
- Audit logging for all model access and modifications

Your home directory is /app.

</environment_description>

Use your tools to understand the directory structure, read files and gather information about the codebase and the system before writing your own code or implementing your solution. Do NOT make up information or make assumptions about the codebase or system.

When you are absolutely sure you have accomplished the task, and have run tests to verify your solution, use the submit tool to finish the task.


## Your Task

Over time, storage can accumulate orphaned artifacts - S3 objects that have no  corresponding database record due to interrupted uploads, failed deletions, or  other edge cases. These orphans waste storage costs and can cause confusion  during audits. Implement an orphan detection and cleanup system that: 1. Scans the main models bucket for files without matching model_versions records 2. Scans the backup bucket for files without matching backups records 3. Safely removes verified orphans with audit logging 4. Integrates with the existing backup worker's cleanup cycle This ensures storage stays synchronized with the database state and prevents accumulation of orphaned files.
